name: Telegram Alerts
description: Push Dispatcharr channel/stream/VOD events to a Telegram chat via a bot. Includes a manual test action, per-event toggles, and an optional cron-driven daily report (public IP + geo + speedtest + activity + source health).
author: R3XCHRIS

fields.label: About
fields.description:
Setup:
  1. Create a bot with @BotFather on Telegram, copy the token.
  2. Send any message to your bot (or add it to a group).
  3. Visit https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates and copy chat.id.
  4. Paste both below, Save, then Actions → Send Test Message.

Docs: https://github.com/R3XCHRIS/telegram-alerts

fields.label: Bot Token (REQUIRED)
fields.help_text: Telegram bot token from @BotFather. Masked in logs.

fields.label: Chat ID (REQUIRED)
fields.help_text: Numeric chat ID. Find via /getUpdates after messaging the bot. Group IDs are negative.

fields.label: Instance Label
fields.help_text: Prefixed in every message, e.g. '[Yoda]'. Useful when multiple Dispatcharr instances send to the same chat.

fields.label: [ALERTS] Toggles
fields.description: Pick which channel/stream events generate Telegram messages.

fields.label: Alert on Channel Start
fields.help_text: Off by default — can be noisy with many channels.

fields.label: Alert on Channel Stop
fields.help_text: Off by default — can be noisy.

fields.label: Alert on Channel Reconnect
fields.help_text: On by default — usually a useful warning signal for flaky upstreams.

fields.label: Alert on Stream Switch
fields.help_text: Off by default — fires whenever a stream URL is swapped.

fields.label: Alert on VOD Start
fields.help_text: Off by default — fires every time a movie/episode starts playing. Can be chatty in multi-user setups.

fields.label: Alert on VOD Stop
fields.help_text: Off by default — fires every time VOD playback ends.

fields.label: [ENRICHMENT]
fields.description: Optionally include extra context per alert. Each toggle adds one DB lookup per event.

fields.label: Include Stream Source
fields.help_text: Add the M3U account name (the channel's first configured stream's source) to each alert.

fields.label: Include Current EPG Program
fields.help_text: Add the currently-airing program title from EPG data to each alert. Requires the channel to have an EPG mapping.

fields.label: Message Format
fields.help_text: HTML uses Telegram's HTML parse mode. Plain disables formatting.

fields.label: [DAILY REPORT]
fields.description: Optional cron-driven digest. Stats window = since previous report, so a weekly cron gets a week of stats.

fields.label: Enable Daily Report
fields.help_text: Master toggle. When on, click 'Apply Schedule' below to register the cron.

fields.label: Report Schedule (cron)
fields.help_text: 5-field cron: 'minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week'. Default '0 9 * * *' = every day at 09:00 in the timezone below. Used by Apply Schedule.

fields.label: Report Timezone
fields.help_text: IANA timezone name (e.g. Europe/London, Australia/Brisbane, America/New_York). Blank = interpret cron as UTC. Re-click Apply Schedule after changing.

fields.label: Report Chat ID (optional)
fields.placeholder: leave blank to use main Chat ID
fields.help_text: Send the daily report to a different chat than per-event alerts. Leave blank to use the main Chat ID.

fields.label: Include Network Section
fields.help_text: Public IP + geographic location lookup.

fields.label: Include Speedtest
fields.help_text: Down/up bandwidth measurement via Cloudflare (~150 MB per test). Only runs when the Network section is also on, and respects the cooldown below.

fields.label: Speedtest Cooldown (hours)
fields.help_text: Minimum hours between speedtest runs. Stops hourly crons from burning bandwidth. Other report sections still update every tick.

fields.label: Include Activity Section
fields.help_text: Channel plays, top channels, VOD plays, errors, stream switches since the previous report.

fields.label: Include Sources Section
fields.help_text: M3U account count and EPG source freshness.

actions.label: [ALERT] Send test message
actions.description: Send a test message to the configured chat. Verifies token, chat ID, and formatting.

actions.label: [REPORT] Send report now
actions.description: Build and send a daily report immediately. Window = since the previous report.

actions.label: [REPORT] Apply / Update schedule
actions.description: Register or update the cron task. Re-click after changing any report setting.

actions.label: [REPORT] Show schedule status
actions.description: Show registered cron, last run, total runs.

confirm.title: Remove daily report schedule?
confirm.message: This unregisters the periodic task. You can re-create it any time with Apply.

actions.label: Handle channel/stream/VOD event (internal)
actions.description: Triggered by Dispatcharr on channel/stream/VOD events. Don't run manually.
